title: handroanthus atractocarpus
intro: Handroanthus atractocarpus, also known as the 'Ipe Roxo', is a tropical tree native to the Amazon rainforest in South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic effects. The health benefits of Handroanthus atractocarpus include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or decoction for medicinal use, and its bark is also used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and extracts.
